name: Neighborhood Housing Services Of Queens, Cdc, Inc
description_short: Non-profit community development corporation providing free financial counseling, housing education, and homebuyer assistance to low-to-moderate-income residents in Queens.
description_long:
Neighborhood Housing Services of Queens (NHSQ) is a community development corporation operating in Queens, New York, with a mission focused on creating and preserving generational wealth for low-to-moderate-income neighbors. The organization serves multiple neighborhoods including Corona, Jackson Heights, Elmhurst, and East Elmhurst in Community Districts 3 and 4, areas that have faced significant challenges including COVID-19 impacts and Hurricane Ida flooding.

NHSQ offers comprehensive services centered on housing stability and financial empowerment. Their primary offerings include first-time homebuyer programs, financial and housing workshops, homeowner support services, and job-seeking assistance. In 2024 alone, they served over 3,650 households and secured more than $50 million in affordable financing for clients. The organization operates a client portal system for case management and regularly hosts both in-person and virtual educational events.

What distinguishes NHSQ is their hyper-local community focus and track record of serving neighborhoods during crisis periods. Rather than operating regionally, they concentrate resources on specific Queens communities, understanding the unique challenges these areas face. Their work is validated through client testimonials, with documented success stories from first-time homebuyers who credit the organization's expertise with making homeownership possible during difficult economic times.

As a non-profit offering free educational services, NHSQ has inherent limitations in scope and capacity compared to for-profit firms. Their services appear primarily education and counseling-focused rather than direct lending or credit repair. Geographic service limitations mean they serve only specific Queens neighborhoods, not all New York residents. However, for their target population in those communities, they represent an accessible, credible resource with demonstrated commitment to client outcomes.
pros: Served 3,650+ households in 2024 with documented success in affordable housing access
Secured $50 million+ in affordable financing for clients, demonstrating tangible lending connections
Non-profit structure with no financial product sales pressure or predatory lending risks
Operates client portal for organized case management and ongoing support
Conducted 2,100+ attendees at financial and housing workshops in 2024
Located in underserved Queens neighborhoods with deep community roots and cultural competency
Free services model removes barriers to access for low-to-moderate-income households
cons: Limited to specific Queens neighborhoods (Corona, Jackson Heights, Elmhurst, East Elmhurst) with no broader service area mentioned
Website does not specify HUD certification, NFCC affiliation, or formal counselor credentials despite categorization as free-help
Appears primarily education and counseling-focused with no indication of direct lending products or credit repair services
No published fee schedule, wait times, or clear service intake timeline documented on website
Limited detail on specific homebuyer program requirements, qualification standards, or financing terms
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Corona, Jackson Heights, Elmhurst, or East Elmhurst neighborhoods seeking free education and financing connections
Low-to-moderate-income residents needing community-based financial counseling and housing stability support
Homeowners seeking to remain in gentrifying neighborhoods and access preservation resources
Job seekers in Queens needing employment assistance paired with financial literacy
diagnosis: NHSQ is best for first-time homebuyers and low-to-moderate-income households in specific Queens neighborhoods seeking free, non-commercial housing education and affordable financing connections. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to Corona, Jackson Heights, Elmhurst, and East Elmhurst—residents outside these areas cannot access services—and the website lacks detail on formal counselor certifications or specific program eligibility requirements.
services: First-time homebuyer counseling and education programs
Housing workshops and community education events
Homeowner support and housing stability services
Job seeking assistance and employment support
Affordable financing connections and facilitation ($50M+ secured)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
